commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a one-shot `onBlockReply` tool-plan announcement before the first non-messaging tool execution in embedded chat sessions, with state and tests for ordering, replay, and delivery accounting.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a strict bug reproduction: this is a feature/UX change. Source inspection of current main shows the tool-start path has no same-medium plan reply, and the PR tests exercise the proposed ordering.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor real-behavior proof plus maintainer UX judgment, not a narrow ClawSweeper repair defect.

target.title: Announce tool plans in chat replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a one-shot, concise tool-plan announcement before the first non-messaging tool execution in embedded chat sessions.
- Deliver that announcement through the same `onBlockReply` chat medium before buffered assistant text is flushed, then immediately continue normal tool execution.
- Track the announcement as a visible deterministic side effect so replay and terminal delivery accounting stay consistent.

## Root Cause

Before this change, `handleToolExecutionStart` only flushed any buffered assistant text and emitted tool progress. There was no deterministic source-channel reply that explained the imminent tool use before tool execution started, so chat users could see tool activity begin without a concise same-medium plan message. The inspected code path was `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts`, where `tool_execution_start` went directly from block-buffer flushing to `onBlockReplyFlush` and tool-start events.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A chat-triggered embedded run that requires a non-messaging tool now sends a concise plan announcement to the same block-reply chat medium before tool progress, buffered text flush, or execution phase callbacks.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in `/home/vac/openclaw-tool-call-plan`, Node via repo wrappers, branch `tool-call-plan-chat-reply`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.calls-onblockreplyflush-before-tool-execution-start-preserve.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.suppresses-message-end-block-replies-message-tool.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`

Evidence after fix: `Test Files  6 passed (6)` and `Tests  104 passed (104)`.

Observed result after fix: The new tests observe `onBlockReply` receiving `I'll run the needed command with OpenClaw tools, then continue with the request.` before `onExecutionPhase`, before buffered text delivery, and only once per run; the existing message-tool suppression test remains green.

What was not tested: Broad remote Testbox/Crabbox proof was blocked before execution because Testbox warmup did not return a `tbx_` id, and direct AWS Crabbox could not refresh credentials from EC2 IMDS in this environment.

## What was not tested

- Broad remote Testbox/Crabbox proof was blocked before execution because Testbox warmup did not return a `tbx_` id, and direct AWS Crabbox could not refresh credentials from EC2 IMDS in this environment.
- A live external chat-channel round trip was not run; the closest feasible behavior proof is the embedded session block-reply harness covering same-medium ordering and message-tool suppression.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.types.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.ts src/agents/pi-tool-handler-state.test-helpers.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.calls-onblockreplyflush-before-tool-execution-start-preserve.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.calls-onblockreplyflush-before-tool-execution-start-preserve.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.suppresses-message-end-block-replies-message-tool.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json` (core + coreTests)
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch` -> `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`
